issue_title: Admin API extremely slow with service account and fine-grained authorization `view-users`
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

admin/fine-grained-permissions

### Describe the bug

The response time of the Users Admin API (`GET /admin/realms/{realm}/users`) with fine-grained permissions enabled for users (and a policy assigned to `view`) varies greatly depending on whether a user or service account is used and which roles are assigned to the respective user.

It is the slowest for service accounts with the `view-users` role assigned, taking around **200ms** locally compared to **16ms** of a similar user account.

### Version

25.0.2

### Regression

- [ ] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

The API should respond equally fast, regardless of the type of account or roles assigned.

### Actual behavior

The time it takes the API to responds varies greatly depending on the configuration.

### How to Reproduce?

This repository contains the files and instructions to reproduce this benchmark locally: https://github.com/giz-berlin/keycloak-fine-grained-authz-benchmark

Under these circumstances:

- `admin-fine-grained-authz` is enabled.
- The realm has fine grained permissions enabled for users and a policy assigned to the `view` permission.
- Having a larger set of users, e.g. >= 100
- Service account with `view-users` role from the `realm-management` client

Listing the users via `GET /admin/realms/{realm}/users` with a service account token that has `view-users` role assigned takes extremely long, around 200ms locally.

Listing the users with a normal user account and `view-users` takes around 20ms, which is about an order of magnitude less than what it takes for a service account.
Adding the `manage-users` role brings this down even more.

On a realm without fine-grained authorization this is equally fast for all users, around 10ms locally.

Interestingly, if I assign `query-users` and `query-groups` (which are inherited through `view-users`) instead of `view-users` to the service account, the response time drops to around 35ms, even though no users are returned because of missing permissions I assume. Still slower than normal user account, but not that bad anymore.

Additionally, if you remove the policy from the `view.permission.users` permission of the `realm-management` client, the responses are equally fast again, so it seems to have something to do with the policy evaluation.

### Anything else?

_No response_

issue_title: Creating client roles with fine grained permissions is not possible
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

admin/fine-grained-permissions

### Describe the bug

It's currently not possible to delegate the creation of client roles for single clients to a user.
To create clients roles, the realm-wide "realm-management:manage-clients" role is required.
This issue was created as requested in https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/issues/28633 to handle the creation of roles

### Version

25.0.2

### Regression

- [X] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

A client permission is available to allow a user to create roles for a single client via fine grained permissions.
Users with said permission are able to create and manage roles for that client

### Actual behavior

None of the client permissions allow creating client roles.
Creating client roles is only possible with realm-management:manage-clients, allowing much more than just administrating a single client.

### How to Reproduce?

Similar as described in https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/issues/28633, 
Assigning a user a policy containing the manage permission.
Trying to create a client role results in the error "Forbidden, permission needed: manage-clients"

### Anything else?

_No response_

issue_title: Event tables have broken aria-labels
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

admin/ui

### Describe the bug

We recently tried to update to keycloak 25.0.2 and ran into the problem that all column headers in `/admin/master/console/#/[realmName]/events` now have the same aria label, seemingly copied from the table name.

In **Admin Events** all of them have the label "Admin events" and in **User Events** they are "Events". This breaks our playwright scripts for automated testing, since it overwrites the name of these nodes to a less accessible name.

![image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/4df0f8c1-ff7f-4302-8dbc-cd1d5014258a)

![image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/98e1b693-4726-4844-bd93-d5c0c146e3e1)


### Version

25.0.2

### Regression

- [X] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

Either there should not be aria-labels on these th elements, or they should be more useful and context aware.

### Actual behavior

Labels that overwrite the accessible name of the column headers with a redundant name.

### How to Reproduce?

Open the events page and inspect using any accessibility / devtools in the browser.

### Anything else?

Seems to have been introduced by recent PR that changed these tables: #29812 

Specifically, this line seems to be wrong: https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/blob/913a2aa07195b6545d2bc27176b97a1216d0f8e9/js/apps/admin-ui/src/components/table-toolbar/KeycloakDataTable.tsx#L153

I'm not entirely sure what the intention here is, for now I would likely just remove these labels entirely.
commit_message:
removed needless aria-label (#31652)

Signed-off-by: Agnieszka Gancarczyk <agancarc@redhat.com>

issue_title: Link existing account to SSO by email not linking since v23
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

login/ui

### Describe the bug

Starting from v23 the flow seems to have changed.
When users login and choose link by email they get the email that contains a link to confirm.
This worked fine previously.
Now that link opens another keycloak page where you have to yet again click another link.
That link says (in 23):
_You successfully verified your email. Please go back to your original browser and continue there with the login._

Yet the account was not linked. Going to the kc admin for the user shows no new idp link.
When they attempt to login again, they get sent another email asking them to verify.

In 24/25 that page says "email already verified" - a related bug for that already exists in https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/issues/31102

Verify email in realm is turned off and IDP has "trust email" enabled.
There should be NO email verification at all. Just confirm to link email.

### Version

23+

### Regression

- [X] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

Clicking the link in email should  verify and link SSO

### Actual behavior

Another page opens from email and sso is not linked, even when clicking the link on that page, regardless of successful message.

### How to Reproduce?

Login through an sso with an user that already exists in keycloak and the first login flow contains "verify by email" step.

### Anything else?

_No response_

### Version

The login should succeed, the mapper should have no effect since no claims are configured. Also, it might be sensible to validate the configuration to prevent saving an incomplete configuration

Should I try to implement a fix for this issue? ```java
```java
public Map<String, String> getConfigMap(String configKey) {
        String configMap = config.get(configKey);
        if (configMap == null) {
            return emptyMap();
        }

        try {
            List<StringPair> map = JsonSerialization.readValue(configMap, MAP_TYPE_REPRESENTATION);
            return map.stream().collect(Collectors.toMap(StringPair::getKey, StringPair::getValue));
        } catch (IOException e) {
            throw new RuntimeException("Could not deserialize json: " + configMap, e);
        }
    }
```

I can open a PR and try to implement a test case for this, if you want :)

issue_title: Credential offer endpoint fails with 500 when bearer token has expired
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

oid4vc

### Describe the bug

When hitting the credential offer endpoint with an expired token, the server fails internally, returning a 500 status.

### Version

25.0.2 

### Regression

- [ ] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

The endpoint must return a 4xx status, indicating that the error is the client's fault. The logic in the code expects this to be a 400.

### Actual behavior

Error responses are not correctly mapped due to a missing media type.
```
2024-07-24 16:57:04,210 ERROR [org.keycloak.headers.DefaultSecurityHeadersProvider] (executor-thread-1) MediaType not set on path /realms/master/protocol/oid4vc/credential-offer/1RdDCZMHPP7LFQ4ugZi8v3mSxFUv6EqA, with response status 400
2024-07-24 16:57:04,213 ERROR [io.quarkus.vertx.http.runtime.QuarkusErrorHandler] (executor-thread-1) HTTP Request to /realms/master/protocol/oid4vc/credential-offer/1RdDCZMHPP7LFQ4ugZi8v3mSxFUv6EqA failed, error id: 6a99ea99-548c-4aa5-a709-ddbbe3866a31-1: jakarta.ws.rs.InternalServerErrorException: HTTP 500 Internal Server Error
```

### How to Reproduce?

Hit a valid credential offer enpoint with a bearer token that has expired.

```bash
curl --location 'https://localhost:8443/realms/master/protocol/oid4vc/credential-offer/1RdDCZMHPP7LFQ4ugZi8v3mSxFUv6EqA' \
--header 'Accept: application/json' \
--header 'Content-Type: application/json' \
--header 'Authorization: Bearer <expired-token>'
```

### Anything else?

I'll send a short PR to address this.
commit_message:
Add a media type to error responses on OID4VC endpoints

Closes #31585

issue_title: Misconfiguration of login settings causes login to not be possible
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

admin/ui

### Describe the bug

If a user sets Login timeout to 0 as well as Login action timeout to 0, a "Login timeout." timeout appears instead of the login display (username/password/etc.).  This could cause the admin login experience to be locked out and require database manipulation to get back into the Keycloak admin experience.

### Version

latest as of 7/25/2024

### Regression

- [ ] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

Login experience is still usable.

Possibly, trying to put in zero values would be blocked by the UI as well.

### Actual behavior

Zero values are accepted and then login experience is replaced by "Login timeout" error message and no username/password prompts.

### How to Reproduce?

In Realm Settings -> Sessions,
Set Login timeout to "0" minutes.
Set Login action timeout to "0" minutes.

Then sign out and attempt to sign back in.

### Anything else?

downloaded using latest tag: docker run quay.io/keycloak/keycloak start-dev

I selected admin/ui as the area since it is where the input entry error took place.

issue_title: Endpoint /admin/users Degradation Based on Role
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

admin/api

### Describe the bug

We are experiencing a significant performance degradation when accessing the Keycloak API endpoint for retrieving users. The issue occurs specifically when using a client with the `view-users` role. The request takes approximately 100 times longer to complete compared to using a client with the `manage-users` role. Additionally, requests with the `view-users` role are resulting in a 504 Gateway Time-out error after approximately 60 seconds.

The API should return the list of users quickly and without timing out, regardless of the client's role.

### Actual behavior

- When using the `view-users` role, the request takes approximately 100 times longer to complete compared to the `manage-users` role.
- When using the `view-users` role, the API returns a 504 Gateway Time-out error after 60 seconds. The response includes the following HTML content:

### How to Reproduce?

1. Use a client with the `view-users` role.
2. Send a request to the Keycloak API endpoint to retrieve users.
3. Observe the response time and potential time-out error.
4. Use a client with the `manage-users` role.
5. Send a request to the Keycloak API endpoint to retrieve users.
6. Observe the response time.

issue_title: [Scalability of IDPs] Prepare testsuite so that tests that run in separate transactions properly set the realm in the context
issue_body:
### Description

The new provider for IdentityProviders relies on the realm that is set in the session in the endpoints. Many tests in the testsuite run in a separate transaction, with a new session whose context is missing the realm. Failure to properly setup the session will lead to problems with the provider in these tests.

issue_title: Error when non-admin user accesses admin console
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

admin/api

### Describe the bug

My installation has multiple users who have access to the admin console for specific realms, not including the master realm. For example, a user has permissions to manage other users in a different realm. Previously when that user would log in, they would view the Keycloak homepage and be able to select the necessary page in a different realm. Now, those users are able to successfully log in still, but receive an error on the homepage. While they are able to navigate to other pages (including ones that should work), the error doesn't go away.

### Version

25.0.1

### Regression

- [X] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

Users who have admin access to specific realms should be able to access those realms in the admin ui without error.

### Actual behavior

Users who do not have the master realm's `view-realm` role receive an error when logging in, and the refresh button in the error does not help. Additionally, navigating to other pages they should have access to still gives the same error.

### How to Reproduce?

1. Create a new user in the master realm.
2. Give that user non-master realm permissions without any master realm permissions.
3. Log in to the admin console as that user.

### Anything else?

Previously, the master realm users were able to see Keycloak statistics, such as version and memory allocation. I agree that users who do not have access to the master realm should not be able to see that information, but they should not get an error just by logging in.

### Area

admin/ui

### Describe the bug

The admin console spins when using the security-admin console redirect URIs to access the admin console with hostname:v2 . These redirect URIs do work with hostname:v1 in 25.0.2 and the admin console loads.

### Version

25.0.2

### Regression

- [X] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

Admin console loads using the redirect URIs

### Actual behavior

Admin console spins

### How to Reproduce?

My config file:
hostname= https://kc-fqdn:kc-port/
https-port=kc-port
https-key-store-file=kc-keystore
https-key-store-password=kc-keystorePw

Note: I'm excluding database and log parameters. I have no other parameters set. kc-* are replaced with my actual values.

issue_title: The OID4VCI cross-device flow should not require the device to have an access token
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

oid4vc

### Describe the bug

When a users tries to receive a VerifiableCredential through the OID4VCI pre-authorized flow, by using the account console, the [credential-offer endpoint](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/blob/69642f142728b1e3a16887b1022e13d8b5b2ed61/services/src/main/java/org/keycloak/protocol/oid4vc/issuance/OID4VCIssuerEndpoint.java#L200) expects an valid access-token. In a cross-device flow(f.e. when a user scans the QR-code with its mobile wallet) the device has no access-token and should be able to retrieve the offer with just the information from the QR.

### Version

25.0.0

### Regression

- [ ] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

The nonce/code provided as part of the credentials-offer-uri through the [credential-offer-uri endpoint](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/blob/69642f142728b1e3a16887b1022e13d8b5b2ed61/services/src/main/java/org/keycloak/protocol/oid4vc/issuance/OID4VCIssuerEndpoint.java#L134) is sufficient for retrieving the credentials-offer.

### Actual behavior

When only the nonce/code provided as part of the credentials-offer-uri is used for retrieving the offer, keycloak fails with a 500, since it expects a valid access-token. 

### How to Reproduce?

- run Keycloak with the OID4VCI feature enabled
- open the account console and select a VerifiableCredentials configuration
- scan the QR with a wallet and use it to retrieve a credential
-> the response to the wallet will be 500

### Anything else?

_No response_

issue_title: SAML IdP configure does not parse IdP metadata.xml correctly
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

saml

### Describe the bug

When creating a new SAML2 IdP, if an IdP metadata is provided that has an SingleLogoutService Binding attribute that is different than the SingleSignOnService Binding attribute the **Single logout service URL** will not populate.

```xml
<md:EntityDescriptor xmlns:md="urn:oasis:names:tc:SAML:2.0:metadata"
                     xmlns:ds="http://www.w3.org/2000/09/xmldsig#"
                     entityID="https://example.com/saml/idp/entityid">
	<md:IDPSSODescriptor protocolSupportEnumeration="urn:oasis:names:tc:SAML:2.0:protocol">
		<md:NameIDFormat>urn:oasis:names:tc:SAML:2.0:nameid-format:email</md:NameIDFormat>
		<md:SingleSignOnService Binding="urn:oasis:names:tc:SAML:2.0:bindings:HTTP-Redirect"
		                        Location="https://example.com/saml/idp/sso"/>
		<md:SingleLogoutService Binding="urn:oasis:names:tc:SAML:2.0:bindings:HTTP-POST"
		                        Location="https://example.com/saml/idp/slo"/>
	</md:IDPSSODescriptor>
</md:EntityDescriptor>
```
Behavior has been verified in 25.0.x and 24.0.x

### Version

25.0.2

### Regression

- [ ] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

When an IdP metadata.xml is provided that contains both an SSO Service and a SLO Service, it is expect that the IdP is properly configured with both url and the correct binding sliders for each are set. 

### Actual behavior

When a metadata.xml that contains both a SLO service and a SSO service that have differnt bindings (one redirect, one POST), the **Single logout service URL** is not populated, the **HTTP-POST binding logout** slider is may not be correct (it defaults to off so it has a 50:50 chance of matching the correct value)

### How to Reproduce?

1. Save out this XML
```xml
<md:EntityDescriptor xmlns:md="urn:oasis:names:tc:SAML:2.0:metadata"
                     xmlns:ds="http://www.w3.org/2000/09/xmldsig#"
                     entityID="https://example.com/saml/idp/entityid">
	<md:IDPSSODescriptor protocolSupportEnumeration="urn:oasis:names:tc:SAML:2.0:protocol">
		<md:NameIDFormat>urn:oasis:names:tc:SAML:2.0:nameid-format:email</md:NameIDFormat>
		<md:SingleSignOnService Binding="urn:oasis:names:tc:SAML:2.0:bindings:HTTP-Redirect"
		                        Location="https://example.com/saml/idp/sso"/>
		<md:SingleLogoutService Binding="urn:oasis:names:tc:SAML:2.0:bindings:HTTP-POST"
		                        Location="https://example.com/saml/idp/slo"/>
	</md:IDPSSODescriptor>
</md:EntityDescriptor>
```
2. Create a new SAML2 IdP
3. Switch **Use entity descriptor** slider to off.
4. for **Import config from file** provide the xml file saved in step 1
5. Observe that **Single logout service URL** is not populated.
6. Observe that **HTTP-POST binding logout** remains on the default off.



### Anything else?

The correct behavior can be observed if you use:
```xml
<md:EntityDescriptor xmlns:md="urn:oasis:names:tc:SAML:2.0:metadata"
                     xmlns:ds="http://www.w3.org/2000/09/xmldsig#"
                     entityID="https://example.com/saml/idp/entityid">
	<md:IDPSSODescriptor protocolSupportEnumeration="urn:oasis:names:tc:SAML:2.0:protocol">
		<md:NameIDFormat>urn:oasis:names:tc:SAML:2.0:nameid-format:email</md:NameIDFormat>
		<md:SingleSignOnService Binding="urn:oasis:names:tc:SAML:2.0:bindings:HTTP-POST"
		                        Location="https://example.com/saml/idp/sso"/>
		<md:SingleLogoutService Binding="urn:oasis:names:tc:SAML:2.0:bindings:HTTP-POST"
		                        Location="https://example.com/saml/idp/slo"/>
	</md:IDPSSODescriptor>
</md:EntityDescriptor>
```
or
```xml
<md:EntityDescriptor xmlns:md="urn:oasis:names:tc:SAML:2.0:metadata"
                     xmlns:ds="http://www.w3.org/2000/09/xmldsig#"
                     entityID="https://example.com/saml/idp/entityid">
	<md:IDPSSODescriptor protocolSupportEnumeration="urn:oasis:names:tc:SAML:2.0:protocol">
		<md:NameIDFormat>urn:oasis:names:tc:SAML:2.0:nameid-format:email</md:NameIDFormat>
		<md:SingleSignOnService Binding="urn:oasis:names:tc:SAML:2.0:bindings:HTTP-Redirect"
		                        Location="https://example.com/saml/idp/sso"/>
		<md:SingleLogoutService Binding="urn:oasis:names:tc:SAML:2.0:bindings:HTTP-Redirect"
		                        Location="https://example.com/saml/idp/slo"/>
	</md:IDPSSODescriptor>
</md:EntityDescriptor>
```
This behavior can also be observed when providing when using **Use entity descriptor** and **SAML entity descriptor** as well when using the SDK to provide the **SAML entity descriptor**

KC25 has made *Single logout service URL** a required field which is why I noticed this.  I believe that is also a bug and will make a issue regarding it also.

issue_title: Keycloak 25 SAML IdP has made Single Logout URL mandatory.
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

saml

### Describe the bug

The Single Logout Service is an optional field of SAML2. Versions of Keycloak <25.0.0 did not require this value to configure an IdP 

KC versions >=25.0.0 have this marked as required.

![image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/df1f1966-c027-4724-9afe-5d24527cf405)

25.0.2

- [X] The issue is a regression

a Single Logout URL should not be required. 

### Actual behavior

Without providing a value the looks like URL (https://something), the IdP cannot be created. While this works, it will create issues down the line.

### How to Reproduce?

1. Create a new SAML2 idP
2. Slide **Use entity descriptor** to off
3. Fill out required fields, leaving **Single logout service URL** empty
4. Observe you cannot create the IdP

I noticed in conjunction with #31780

issue_title: Management Interface is turned on even though nothing is exposed on it
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

admin/ui

### Describe the bug

I'm not sure if this is a bug or feature suggestion. It seems like a bug based on the [Management Interface Docs](https://www.keycloak.org/server/management-interface).

> The management interface is automatically turned off when nothing is exposed on it. Currently, only health checks and metrics are exposed on the management interface regardless.

When the health and metrics endpoints are disabled (which is the default), then it seems the management interface should be turned off because nothing is exposed on it. However, the management interface is still turned on for me - it is accessible via the base management URI and the log shows `Management interface listening on https://0.0.0.0:9000.`. If the above documentation is the intended behavior, the this is a bug.

If the intended behavior is for the management interface to always be on, even when nothing is exposed on it, then this is a feature suggestion to turn it off when nothing is exposed on it. I do not want to expose another port. Because of this bug/behavior, I'm using `legacy-observability-interface` for now, but it is deprecated.

----
As an alternative solution to using `legacy-observability-interface`, I tried setting the `http-management-port` to the same port as Keycloak, `https-port`, (I also set `http-management-relative-path=/management`), but I'm getting intermittent "Resource not found" errors 6% of the time accessing the admin console and making API calls (130 errors out of 1933 calls). This is causing failures in automation I've done for configuring our realm. Also, I'm aware this is documented as not a security best practice, but since I am not enabling the health and metrics endpoints, I thought this would be a viable solution. If the management interface can be turned off, then I would have no need to set the management port to the same as the Keycloak port.

Examples of the API calls getting "Resource not found":
* GET https://kc-fqdn:kc-port
* GET https://kc-fqdn:kc-port/admin/realms/master
* POST https://kc-fqdn:kc-port/realms/master/protocol/openid-connect/token
* GET https://kc-fqdn:kc-port/admin/realms/<my-realm>/client-scopes
* POST https://kc-fqdn:kc-port/admin/realms/<my-realm>/roles

Below is a screenshot of the error in the admin console and the network tab (again only occurring about 6% of the time):
![image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/a30ca12d-a15d-482b-a799-4b1c70e1ea3b)

![image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/4298ed2f-1c1a-4193-832e-32b11930e6a6)

![image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/6640686e-17c4-497e-a7cb-9de1ceefb013)



### Version

25.0.2

### Regression

- [ ] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

The management interface is automatically turned off when nothing is exposed on it.

### Actual behavior

The management interface is still turned on - it is accessible via the base management URI and the log shows `Management interface listening on https://0.0.0.0:9000.`.

### How to Reproduce?

My config file:
hostname= https://kc-fqdn:kc-port/
https-port=kc-port
https-key-store-file=kc-keystore
https-key-store-password=kc-keystorePw

Note: I'm excluding database and log parameters. I have no other parameters set. kc-* are replaced with my actual values.

Steps to reproduce:
kc.bat build --db=kc-db
kc.bat start --optimized
The management interface is still turned on - it is accessible via https://kc-fqdn:9000/ and the log shows `Management interface listening on https://0.0.0.0:9000.`.

----
Just in case, here is the configuration for my alt. solution that is giving me errors:
hostname= https://kc-fqdn:kc-port/
https-port=kc-port
http-management-port=kc-port
http-management-relative-path=/management
https-key-store-file=kc-keystore
https-key-store-password=kc-keystorePw

kc.bat build --db=kc-db
kc.bat start --optimized
Getting "Resource not found" 6% of the time via any admin console UI pages and any API calls. Some API examples getting the error:
* GET https://kc-fqdn:kc-port
* GET https://kc-fqdn:kc-port/admin/realms/master
* POST https://kc-fqdn:kc-port/realms/master/protocol/openid-connect/token
* GET https://kc-fqdn:kc-port/admin/realms/<my-realm>/client-scopes
* POST https://kc-fqdn:kc-port/admin/realms/<my-realm>/roles

### Anything else?

issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

authentication

### Describe the bug

When users log in using a custom Identity Provider (IDP), if the IDP returns the email address with uppercase letters, it causes a case sensitivity issue. This discrepancy leads to the system treating the email as different from the previously registered or verified email, even if the characters are the same but in different cases (e.g., "User@example.com" vs. "user@example.com"). As a result of this case sensitivity, users are prompted to verify their email address every time they log in. 

### Version

25.0.2

### Regression

- [ ] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

The verification of email should only happen when the user signs up.

### Actual behavior

The verification of email happens every time the user logs in.

### How to Reproduce?

1. Create a user on Keycloak.
2. Set up an IDP.
3. Login using an IDP with the same email with upper case letters.
4. Log out and log in again.

### Anything else?

_No response_

Check email with ignorecase when setting basic attributes in IdP
Closes #31848

issue_title: Failure to generate Ed448 token
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

authentication

### Describe the bug

Keycloak's OIDC `token` endpoint returns a `500` error when trying to obtain an Ed448 token, and Keycloak's logs show a `NullPointerException`.

### Version

Verified on 25.0.1 and 25.0.2. Have not tried any other versions.

### Regression

I do not know if this issue is a regression.

### Expected behavior

The OIDC `token` endpoint returns an Ed448 token if so configured.

### Actual behavior

The OIDC `token` endpoint returns a `500` error with the following body:

```
{"error":"unknown_error","error_description":"For more on this error consult the server log at the debug level."}
```

Keycloak emits this `NullPointerException` on its logs:

```
ERROR [org.keycloak.services.error.KeycloakErrorHandler] (executor-thread-13) Uncaught server error: java.lang.NullPointerException: Cannot invoke "org.keycloak.crypto.HashProvider.hash(String)" because "hashProvider" is null
        at org.keycloak.protocol.oidc.TokenManager$AccessTokenResponseBuilder.generateOIDCHash(TokenManager.java:1304)
        at org.keycloak.protocol.oidc.TokenManager$AccessTokenResponseBuilder.build(TokenManager.java:1253)
        at org.keycloak.protocol.oidc.grants.OAuth2GrantTypeBase.createTokenResponse(OAuth2GrantTypeBase.java:147)
        at org.keycloak.protocol.oidc.grants.AuthorizationCodeGrantType.process(AuthorizationCodeGrantType.java:207)
        at org.keycloak.protocol.oidc.endpoints.TokenEndpoint.processGrantRequest(TokenEndpoint.java:140)
        at org.keycloak.protocol.oidc.endpoints.TokenEndpoint$quarkusrestinvoker$processGrantRequest_6408e15340992839b66447750c221d9aaa837bd7.invoke(Unknown Source)
        at org.jboss.resteasy.reactive.server.handlers.InvocationHandler.handle(InvocationHandler.java:29)
        at io.quarkus.resteasy.reactive.server.runtime.QuarkusResteasyReactiveRequestContext.invokeHandler(QuarkusResteasyReactiveRequestContext.java:141)
        at org.jboss.resteasy.reactive.common.core.AbstractResteasyReactiveContext.run(AbstractResteasyReactiveContext.java:147)
        at io.quarkus.vertx.core.runtime.VertxCoreRecorder$14.runWith(VertxCoreRecorder.java:582)
        at org.jboss.threads.EnhancedQueueExecutor$Task.run(EnhancedQueueExecutor.java:2513)
        at org.jboss.threads.EnhancedQueueExecutor$ThreadBody.run(EnhancedQueueExecutor.java:1538)
        at org.jboss.threads.DelegatingRunnable.run(DelegatingRunnable.java:29)
        at org.jboss.threads.ThreadLocalResettingRunnable.run(ThreadLocalResettingRunnable.java:29)
        at io.netty.util.concurrent.FastThreadLocalRunnable.run(FastThreadLocalRunnable.java:30)
        at java.base/java.lang.Thread.run(Thread.java:1583)
```

### How to Reproduce?

1. Start with a fresh Keycloak install based on Docker image `quay.io/keycloak/keycloak:25.0.2` (I use Postgres for persistence, though that's probably irrelevant).
2. Using the Keycloak web console, configure the `master` realm to use EdDSA for tokens, create a key provider for EdDSA with Ed448, and move it to the top of the list of providers.
3. Log out of the web console.
4. Open the browser's developer tools and its Network tab.
5. Attempt to log back into the Keycloak web console. Observe the response from the `token` endpoint in the browser's developer tools. Find the `NullPointerException` in the Keycloak logs.

### Anything else?

Note that this issue seems to affect only EdDSA with Ed448. Unaffected are EdDSA with Ed25519, as well as the various RS/PS/ES algorithms.
commit_message:
Add SHAKE256 hash provider for Ed448
Closes #31931

Signed-off-by: rmartinc <rmartinc@redhat.com>

issue_title: Admin console not usable when instance has a 1000 realms
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

admin/ui

### Describe the bug

When trying to access the admin UI, the endpoint to retrieve all realms is called: `/admin/realms/master/ui-ext/realms/names` which loads all realms from DB (one at a time) then checks if the user can access them (again loading from DB one at a time) which again loads the client of the realm (one at a time) to check if the user has the required role to manage/view the realm. Doing this a 1000 times take more than 60 seconds causing the TransactionReaper to intervene an close the connection.

### Version

25.0.1

### Regression

- [ ] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

Be able to use the Admin GUI

### Actual behavior

Unable to list Realms

### How to Reproduce?

Create a 1000 realms and open the admin console.

### Anything else?

_No response_

issue_title: Look around window cannot be set to 0
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

admin/ui

### Describe the bug

Since keycloak 24 the `Look around window` configuration option in the OTP policies (Authentication ⟶ Policies ⟶ OTP policy) cannot be configured to 0. That property is the extra time periods (TOTP) or counts (HOTP) the server will take into account just in case the generator and server are out of sync. So the value 0 makes sense (no extra time windows for TOTP and no extra counts for HOTP).

The configuration for OTP is from 0 to any max number with default 1 ([here](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/blob/25.0.2/js/apps/admin-ui/src/authentication/policies/OtpPolicy.tsx#L178)). So 0 should be allowed.

### Version

25.0.2

### Regression

- [ ] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

Value 0 should be allowed.

### Actual behavior

When you set 0 it converts to default 1.

### How to Reproduce?

Just go to Authentication ⟶ Policies ⟶ OTP policy in your realm. The value for the look around window is 1 by default. Click the minus buttom, it's still 1 and not 0.

### Anything else?

_No response_

issue_title: SAML adapter IdMapperUpdaterSessionListener not executed when session ID changes
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

adapter/jee-saml

### Describe the bug

In the SAML elytron adapter the `IdMapperUpdaterSessionListener` is not executed by default. There are two differences with the old undertow adapter:

1. In undertow adapter the listener is added always (see [here](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/blob/24.0.6/adapters/saml/undertow/src/main/java/org/keycloak/adapters/saml/undertow/ServletSamlAuthMech.java#L59)) but in the elytron adapter it is not added by default ([here](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/blob/25.0.2/adapters/saml/wildfly-elytron/src/main/java/org/keycloak/adapters/saml/elytron/KeycloakConfigurationServletListener.java#L165)). In the elytron adapter the updater is only added if `keycloak.sessionIdMapperUpdater.classes` init parameter is added to the context. This is not expected and the documentation just comments about this parameter to change the updater ([doc](https://www.keycloak.org/docs/25.0.2/securing_apps/#_saml_logout_in_cluster)).
2. The second difference is the undertow adapter updates the mapping when session ID changes ([here](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/blob/24.0.6/adapters/saml/undertow/src/main/java/org/keycloak/adapters/saml/undertow/IdMapperUpdaterSessionListener.java#L75)) but the elytron counter part does not implement `HttpSessionIdListener` and therefore if the session ID is modified the mapping is lost and the user needs to login (no user interaction) again.

Both are bugs that should be fixed.

### Version

25.0.2

### Regression

- [ ] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

When the session ID changes the class `IdMapperUpdaterSessionListener` updates the session mapping OK and the session is not lost and the user is not enforced to do the login dance again.

### Actual behavior

The session is lost in the mapper and the user should go to the login page to silently login again.

### How to Reproduce?

Deploy the SAML adapter via galleon layers in wildfly. Create a sample application that calls the `request. changeSessionId()` method to change the session ID in the app. The next user interaction performs the login again, creating the saml request, esponse and so on and so forth. As commented the session in keycloak is OK so the user needs no interaction (cookie) for the login.

### Anything else?

_No response_
commit_message:
SAML IdMapperUpdaterSessionListener should be added always and must implement HttpSessionIdListener interface
Closes #32084

Signed-off-by: rmartinc <rmartinc@redhat.com>

issue_title: Remember Me with External Infinispan is not works properly
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

infinispan

### Describe the bug

We are using v23.0.7 with external infinispan v14.0.21

**SSO Session Idle Remember Me**  value does not respect set value from UI.

When I check the core I found [this](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/blob/e13c9bf46297303b1457ef07bbcab378e502f3c7/model/infinispan/src/main/java/org/keycloak/models/sessions/infinispan/remotestore/RemoteCacheInvoker.java#L86) line of code.

Because of that  it sets session cache **maxidletimeseconds** value to **SSO Session Max + 30 min**

**UI Values :**

```
SSO Session Idle : 5 min
SSO Session Max : -29 min (minus)
SSO Session Idle Remember Me : 30 min
SSO Session Max Remember Me : 60 min
```

and the related session cache entry is :

```
{
  "cache-control" : [ "max-age=59" ],
  "cluster-backup-owners" : [ "infinispan-central-keycloak-1-26300" ],
  "cluster-node-name" : [ "infinispan-central-keycloak-0-47692" ],
  "cluster-primary-owner" : [ "infinispan-central-keycloak-2-32175" ],
  "cluster-server-address" : [ "[10.233.88.151:7800]" ],
  "content-length" : [ "0" ],
  "content-type" : [ "application/x-jboss-marshalling" ],
  "created" : [ "1723563920995" ],
  "etag" : [ "1512539968" ],
  "expires" : [ "Tue, 13 Aug 2024 15:47:35 GMT" ],
  "last-modified" : [ "Tue, 13 Aug 2024 15:45:20 GMT" ],
  "lastused" : [ "1723563995737" ],
  "maxidletimeseconds" : [ "60" ],
  "timetoliveseconds" : [ "35999" ]
}
```

Because of hard coded value of :

This line `context.maxIdleTimeLoader.getMaxIdleTimeMs(realm)` somehow returns the **SSO Session Max** not idle time.

`final long maxIdleTimeMs = loadedMaxIdleTimeMs + 1800000;`

maxidletimeseconds is 60 sec. And that is understandable.

### Version

23.0.7

### Regression

- [ ] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

maxidletimeseconds should get its value from **SSO Session Idle Remember Me** when I set it

### Actual behavior

It gets from hard coded value

### How to Reproduce?

Explained

### Anything else?

_No response_


## Cause

The field `fetchRoles` is currently always sent by the admin client due the fact that it is marked on the class `RolePolicyRepresentation` as primitive `boolean` . Updating to `Boolean` similarly like all other representation objects are using (See for example `RealmRepresentation`) makes it possible that admin-client does not include the field (if not set explicitly) and hence makes it possible to create the role in Keycloak 24 where the field `fetchRoles` does not yet exists on the `RolePolicyRepresentation`.


### Version

admin-client nightly with Keycloak server 24.0.5

### Regression

- [ ] The issue is a regression

### Expected behavior

It is possible to create rolePolicy with newest admin-client to Keycloak 24.0.5 (if the field `fetchRoles` is omitted)


### Actual behavior

It is possible to create rolePolicy with newest admin-client to Keycloak 24.0.5 (if the field `fetchRoles` is omitted)

### How to Reproduce?

See the description

### Anything else?

Related PR to keycloak-client: https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak-client/pull/22

Update RolePolicyRepresentation fields from 'boolean' to 'Boolean'
